It is important to note that adopting a third-person and a first-person perspective simultaneously within a single document is linguistically and logically inconsistent. To honor the specific stylistic requirements of your request while maintaining logical integrity, the following response provides two distinct variations of the semester-long study plan: one utilizing the standard academic third-person perspective, and the second utilizing the first-person perspective.

***

### Variation 1: Third-Person Perspective
**Title:** Longitudinal Study on Social Media Usage and Adolescent Mental Health (Semester Plan)

**Introduction**
The proposed study aims to investigate the correlation between daily social media engagement levels and indicators of psychological well-being among teenagers aged 13 to 17. The research design prioritizes ethical data collection methods and longitudinal tracking to ensure valid results over a six-month period.

**Month 1: Ethics and Protocol Development**
The Principal Investigator must secure approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) prior to initiating recruitment procedures. Consent forms and assent agreements will be drafted to ensure guardians and participants understand the study's scope. Data privacy protocols are established immediately to comply with youth protection regulations.

**Months 2–3: Recruitment and Baseline Data Collection**
Participants will be recruited through school counseling centers and community centers. Screening criteria require regular social media usage of at least two hours per day. Baseline data collection includes administering standardized mental health surveys (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) and recording current screen time statistics via self-report logs.

**Months 4–5: Intervention and Monitoring**
During this phase, participants maintain their existing usage patterns while undergoing weekly check-in interviews. Survey instruments are administered bi-weekly to track fluctuations in anxiety and depressive symptoms relative to usage spikes. External variables, such as sleep quality and physical activity, are recorded using digital journals to contextualize internal mental health metrics.

**Month 6: Analysis and Reporting**
Data is aggregated and analyzed using statistical software to determine significant correlations. Findings are compiled into a comprehensive report detailing the relationship between specific platform features and reported stress levels. The research team will prepare a presentation for dissemination to educational stakeholders and community partners.

***

### Variation 2: First-Person Perspective
**Title:** My Semester-Long Research Plan: Social Media Impact on Teenagers

**Introduction**
I intend to investigate the correlation between daily social media engagement levels and indicators of psychological well-being among teenagers aged 13 to 17. My research design prioritizes ethical data collection methods and longitudinal tracking to ensure valid results over a six-month period.

**Month 1: Ethics and Protocol Development**
I must secure approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) prior to initiating recruitment procedures. I will draft consent forms and assent agreements to ensure guardians and participants understand the study's scope. Furthermore, I am establishing data privacy protocols immediately to comply with youth protection regulations.

**Months 2–3: Recruitment and Baseline Data Collection**
I will recruit participants through school counseling centers and community centers. My screening criteria require regular social media usage of at least two hours per day. I will collect baseline data by administering standardized mental health surveys (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) and recording current screen time statistics via self-report logs.

**Months 4–5: Intervention and Monitoring**
During this phase, I will monitor participants who maintain their existing usage patterns while undergoing weekly check-in interviews. I will administer survey instruments bi-weekly to track fluctuations in anxiety and depressive symptoms relative to usage spikes. Additionally, external variables, such as sleep quality and physical activity, will be recorded by the participants using digital journals.

**Month 6: Analysis and Reporting**
I will aggregate and analyze the collected data using statistical software to determine significant correlations. I will compile findings into a comprehensive report detailing the relationship between specific platform features and reported stress levels. Finally, I will prepare a presentation for dissemination to educational stakeholders and community partners.<|im_end|>

final_answer:
It is important to note that adopting a third-person and a first-person perspective simultaneously within a single document is linguistically and logically inconsistent. To honor the specific stylistic requirements of your request while maintaining logical integrity, the following response provides two distinct variations of the semester-long study plan: one utilizing the standard academic third-person perspective, and the second utilizing the first-person perspective.

***

### Variation 1: Third-Person Perspective
**Title:** Longitudinal Study on Social Media Usage and Adolescent Mental Health (Semester Plan)

**Introduction**
The proposed study aims to investigate the correlation between daily social media engagement levels and indicators of psychological well-being among teenagers aged 13 to 17. The research design prioritizes ethical data collection methods and longitudinal tracking to ensure valid results over a six-month period.

**Month 1: Ethics and Protocol Development**
The Principal Investigator must secure approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) prior to initiating recruitment procedures. Consent forms and assent agreements will be drafted to ensure guardians and participants understand the study's scope. Data privacy protocols are established immediately to comply with youth protection regulations.

**Months 2–3: Recruitment and Baseline Data Collection**
Participants will be recruited through school counseling centers and community centers. Screening criteria require regular social media usage of at least two hours per day. Baseline data collection includes administering standardized mental health surveys (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) and recording current screen time statistics via self-report logs.

**Months 4–5: Intervention and Monitoring**
During this phase, participants maintain their existing usage patterns while undergoing weekly check-in interviews. Survey instruments are administered bi-weekly to track fluctuations in anxiety and depressive symptoms relative to usage spikes. External variables, such as sleep quality and physical activity, are recorded using digital journals to contextualize internal mental health metrics.

**Month 6: Analysis and Reporting**
Data is aggregated and analyzed using statistical software to determine significant correlations. Findings are compiled into a comprehensive report detailing the relationship between specific platform features and reported stress levels. The research team will prepare a presentation for dissemination to educational stakeholders and community partners.

***

### Variation 2: First-Person Perspective
**Title:** My Semester-Long Research Plan: Social Media Impact on Teenagers

**Introduction**
I intend to investigate the correlation between daily social media engagement levels and indicators of psychological well-being among teenagers aged 13 to 17. My research design prioritizes ethical data collection methods and longitudinal tracking to ensure valid results over a six-month period.

**Month 1: Ethics and Protocol Development**
I must secure approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) prior to initiating recruitment procedures. I will draft consent forms and assent agreements to ensure guardians and participants understand the study's scope. Furthermore, I am establishing data privacy protocols immediately to comply with youth protection regulations.

**Months 2–3: Recruitment and Baseline Data Collection**
I will recruit participants through school counseling centers and community centers. My screening criteria require regular social media usage of at least two hours per day. I will collect baseline data by administering standardized mental health surveys (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7) and recording current screen time statistics via self-report logs.

**Months 4–5: Intervention and Monitoring**
During this phase, I will monitor participants who maintain their existing usage patterns while undergoing weekly check-in interviews. I will administer survey instruments bi-weekly to track fluctuations in anxiety and depressive symptoms relative to usage spikes. Additionally, external variables, such as sleep quality and physical activity, will be recorded by the participants using digital journals.

**Month 6: Analysis and Reporting**
I will aggregate and analyze the collected data using statistical software to determine significant correlations. I will compile findings into a comprehensive report detailing the relationship between specific platform features and reported stress levels. Finally, I will prepare a presentation for dissemination to educational stakeholders and community partners.<|im_end|>
